The provided Node.js code is vulnerable to SQL injection attacks due to the use of user input directly in SQL queries without proper sanitization. This allows malicious actors to manipulate database queries.

In the Bash script, there are several security concerns. First, the shebang line is missing, which could lead to unexpected behavior across different systems. Second, the script blindly executes user-provided input, potentially allowing code injection.

These vulnerabilities should be addressed promptly to ensure system integrity and data safety. Remember to always prioritize security in your development process.

});

const express = require('express');
const { exec } = require('child_process');
const app = express();
app.get('/logs', (req, res) => {
  const cmd = 'cat /var/log/' + req.query.file;
  exec(cmd, (err, stdout, stderr) => {
    if (err) return res.status(500).send('error');
    res.send(stdout);
  });
});

import javax.crypto.Cipher;
import javax.crypto.spec.SecretKeySpec;
import java.nio.file.*;
import java.util.Base64;

public class CryptoDemo {
  public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception {
    String pwd = "supersecret";
    Files.writeString(Paths.get("users.txt"), "alice:" + pwd + "\n");
    Cipher c = Cipher.getInstance("AES/ECB/NoPadding");
    SecretKeySpec key = new SecretKeySpec("0123456789abcdef".getBytes(), "AES");
    c.init(Cipher.ENCRYPT_MODE, key);
    byte[] out = c.doFinal("topsecretdata____".getBytes());
    System.out.println(Base64.getEncoder().encodeToString(out));
  }
}
